task_completion.description: The agent navigated to the Segment Editor module in 3D Slicer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The module selector (usually top left) shows 'Segment Editor', or the Segment Editor panel is visible on the left side.

task_completion.description: The 'RightLung' segment was selected for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Segment Editor panel, the 'RightLung' row is highlighted/selected in the segment list.

task_completion.description: The Smoothing effect tool was activated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Smoothing' button (icon often looks like a blur or iron) is pressed or highlighted in the Effects list.

task_completion.description: The Smoothing method was set specifically to 'Closing (fill holes)'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Under the Smoothing options, the 'Method' dropdown explicitly displays 'Closing (fill holes)'. This is critical; it must not be 'Gaussian' or 'Median'.

task_completion.description: The kernel size was set to an appropriate value (3-5mm).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Kernel Size slider or input field shows a value between 3.00 and 5.00 mm.

task_completion.description: The segmentation visually changed to fill internal holes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D slice views (Red/Yellow/Green windows), the colored lung segment appears solid. Previous internal dark spots (holes) are now filled with the segment color.

task_completion.description: The agent saved the segmentation to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save Data' dialog is visible, or the 'Export to files' section in Segment Editor is used, targeting the filename 'filled_lung_segment.seg.nrrd'.

integrity.description: The agent used the 3D Slicer GUI and not a terminal/python console script to perform the segmentation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with buttons, sliders, and menus in the Slicer interface, not code entry in the Python Interactor.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual 3D Slicer application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches the standard 3D Slicer layout (3D view, slice views, module panel) and is not a text editor or static image.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply copy a pre-existing file to the destination.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen performing the smoothing operation within the software, rather than just opening a file manager and moving files.
